key_points: The judiciary is the system of courts that interprets laws and delivers justice
It ensures that laws are applied fairly to all citizens
The judiciary protects the Fundamental Rights of people
The Supreme Court is the highest court in India
High Courts work at the state level and District Courts work at the district level
The judiciary helps resolve disputes between individuals, groups or the government
examples: A person can go to court if their Fundamental Rights are violated
The Supreme Court can review decisions of lower courts
Courts can punish people who break the law
Courts settle disputes between citizens or organizations
formulas: Court System → Supreme Court → High Court → District Court
Rule of Law → Everyone equal before the law
exam_tips: Write the definition of judiciary clearly
Remember the hierarchy of courts in India
Mention the role of the judiciary in protecting rights
Give examples of how courts solve disputes
